{
  "name": "local-first-todo",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "vite",
    "build": "tsc --noEmit && vite build",
    "preview": "vite preview",
    "lint": "eslint . --max-warnings=0",
    "test": "jest",
    "test:watch": "jest --watch",
    "test:coverage": "jest --coverage",
    "e2e": "playwright test",
    "bundle:check": "node scripts/check-bundle.mjs"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "idb": "8.0.2",
    "react": "18.3.1",
    "react-dom": "18.3.1",
    "uuid": "11.0.5",
    "zod": "3.24.2",
    "zustand": "5.0.3"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@axe-core/playwright": "4.10.2",
    "@eslint/js": "9.19.0",
    "@playwright/test": "1.50.1",
    "@testing-library/jest-dom": "6.6.3",
    "@testing-library/react": "16.2.0",
    "@testing-library/user-event": "14.6.1",
    "@types/jest": "29.5.14",
    "@types/node": "22.13.1",
    "@types/react": "18.3.18",
    "@types/react-dom": "18.3.5",
    "@types/uuid": "10.0.0",
    "@vitejs/plugin-react": "4.3.4",
    "autoprefixer": "10.4.20",
    "eslint": "9.19.0",
    "eslint-plugin-react-hooks": "5.2.0",
    "eslint-plugin-react-refresh": "0.4.18",
    "fake-indexeddb": "6.0.0",
    "jest": "29.7.0",
    "jest-environment-jsdom": "29.7.0",
    "postcss": "8.5.1",
    "tailwindcss": "3.4.17",
    "ts-jest": "29.2.5",
    "typescript": "5.7.3",
    "typescript-eslint": "8.22.0",
    "vite": "6.1.0"
  },
  "engines": {
    "node": ">=20.11"
  }
}

{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2022",
    "useDefineForClassFields": true,
    "lib": ["ES2022", "DOM", "DOM.Iterable", "WebWorker"],
    "allowJs": false,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "allowSyntheticDefaultImports": true,
    "strict": true,
    "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
    "module": "ESNext",
    "moduleResolution": "Bundler",
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "noEmit": true,
    "jsx": "react-jsx",
    "types": ["vite/client", "jest", "@testing-library/jest-dom"]
  },
  "include": ["src", "vite.config.ts", "jest.config.ts", "playwright.config.ts"]
}

import { v4 as uuidv4 } from 'uuid';

export const createId = (): string => uuidv4();
export default {};

name: CI
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
permissions:
  contents: read
jobs:
  verify:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run lint
      - run: npm run test:coverage -- --runInBand
      - run: npm run build
      - run: npm run bundle:check
      - run: npm audit --audit-level=high
      - name: Verify CSP-compatible HTML
        run: node -e "const fs=require('fs');const h=fs.readFileSync('dist/index.html','utf8');if(/<script(?![^>]*src=)/i.test(h)||/ style=/i.test(h))process.exit(1)"
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist

name: E2E
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
permissions:
  contents: read
jobs:
  playwright:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npx playwright install --with-deps chromium firefox webkit
      - run: npm run e2e
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: always()
        with:
          name: playwright-report
          path: playwright-report
          retention-days: 14
  deploy:
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    needs: playwright
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run build
      - name: Deploy to Netlify
        if: env.NETLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN != '' && env.NETLIFY_SITE_ID != ''
        env:
          NETLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NETLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN }}
          NETLIFY_SITE_ID: ${{ secrets.NETLIFY_SITE_ID }}
        run: npx --yes netlify-cli@17.38.0 deploy --dir=dist --prod --site="$NETLIFY_SITE_ID" --auth="$NETLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN"

node_modules/
dist/
coverage/
playwright-report/
test-results/
.env
.env.*
!.env.example
.DS_Store

# The application requires no runtime environment variables.
# Deployment tokens belong in GitHub Actions Secrets, never in this file.

# Local First Todo

## Project overview

Local First Todo is a single-user React and TypeScript task manager. It runs entirely in the browser, stores tasks and projects in IndexedDB, supports offline app-shell loading, synchronizes mutations between tabs, and provides validated JSON backup and restore.

No account, server, analytics service, or third-party cloud synchronization is used. Browser or operating-system backup features may still copy site data. Clearing browser site data removes the database, so regular exports are recommended.

## Installation

Requirements:

- Node.js 20.11 or newer
- npm 10 or newer
- A current browser with IndexedDB and Web Crypto support

Install and start development:

```bash
npm install
npm run dev
```

Open `http://localhost:5173`.

Run a production build:

```bash
npm run build
npm run preview
```

Commit the generated `package-lock.json` after the first dependency installation. CI uses `npm ci`, so the lockfile is required in source control and must be updated whenever dependencies change.

## Environment variables

The browser application has no runtime environment variables and contains no secrets.

Optional deployment secrets are configured only in GitHub Actions Secrets:

- `NETLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN`: Netlify deployment token
- `NETLIFY_SITE_ID`: destination Netlify site ID

Never place deployment tokens in `.env` files, source code, workflow logs, or client-side Vite variables.

## Development commands

- `npm run dev`: Vite development server
- `npm run lint`: ESLint validation
- `npm test`: Jest and React Testing Library tests
- `npm run test:coverage`: tests with coverage thresholds
- `npm run build`: TypeScript validation and production build
- `npm run bundle:check`: enforce the 1 MiB JavaScript limit
- `npm run e2e`: Playwright tests in Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit
- `npm run preview`: serve the production build locally

Install Playwright browsers before the first E2E run:

```bash
npx playwright install --with-deps chromium firefox webkit
```

## Storage behavior

IndexedDB database name: `todo-app`, version 1.

Object stores:

- `projects`, keyed by UUID with a `name` index
- `tasks`, keyed by UUID with `projectId`, `completed`, and `dueDate` indexes

If IndexedDB cannot be opened, the application uses session-only memory and displays a warning. LocalStorage is never activated automatically; the user must explicitly consent in Settings. LocalStorage has lower capacity and cannot provide native transaction guarantees.

Quota failures produce a friendly remediation message. Export data before clearing browser storage.

## Projects, deletion, and Undo

Project deletion requires explicit confirmation. The default operation unassigns associated tasks. Cascade deletion separately confirms the exact number of tasks affected. Task and project deletion remain undoable for eight seconds by default, configurable from one to thirty seconds in Settings. Final multi-entity project operations use a single IndexedDB transaction.

## Import and export

Settings provides JSON export and import. Exports include:

- schema version
- projects and tasks
- entity counts
- SHA-256 checksum

Imports are validated with zod, reject unknown fields and unsafe keys, verify checksums, and reject ID collisions rather than silently merging records. Export current data before importing. A version that cannot be migrated is rejected while preserving the supplied payload for recovery.

## Optional encryption

Encrypted exports are opt-in. Encryption uses AES-256-GCM with a key derived by PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA-256, a random 128-bit salt, random 96-bit IV, and 310,000 iterations. Encryption metadata is stored with the ciphertext.

A lost passphrase cannot be recovered. Keep the passphrase in a password manager and test restoration before relying on an encrypted backup. Encryption protects exported files; IndexedDB remains governed by browser and operating-system security.

## Offline service worker

The production build registers a minimal service worker that caches same-origin app-shell assets. Network responses are preferred, with cached responses used offline. Updates are installed without forced activation; the UI asks before reloading when an update is ready.

For reliable service-worker behavior, deploy over HTTPS. Localhost is accepted by browsers for development.

## Security headers

Configure the static host with headers equivalent to:

```text
Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self'; img-src 'self' data:; connect-src 'self'; worker-src 'self'; object-src 'none'; base-uri 'none'; frame-ancestors 'none'; form-action 'self'
X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
Referrer-Policy: no-referrer
Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()
Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy: same-origin
```

The application does not use inline scripts, inline style attributes, `innerHTML`, or remote assets. User content is rendered through React as plain text.

## Deployment

Build the static assets:

```bash
npm ci
npm run build
```

Deploy the `dist` directory to Netlify, Vercel, GitHub Pages, or another HTTPS static host. Configure SPA fallback routing to `/index.html`, preserve `/service-worker.js`, and apply the security headers above.

The included GitHub Actions workflows run linting, unit coverage, build verification, bundle checks, npm audit, CSP checks, Playwright browser tests, and optional Netlify deployment. Deployment runs only when the required GitHub Actions Secrets exist.

## Accessibility checklist

Automated axe scans run in Playwright. Before release, manually verify:

- all actions are reachable by keyboard
- focus indicators remain visible
- Settings exposes dialog semantics and closes predictably
- confirmation dialogs describe destructive impact
- toast messages are announced by a live region
- controls retain useful labels at desktop, tablet, and mobile widths
- zoom at 200 percent does not hide task operations

## Privacy and backups

All application data is local to the browser profile. It is not sent to this project or a project-controlled server. Browser synchronization, device backups, managed-device software, extensions, and operating-system backups may copy or inspect local browser data. Use encrypted exports for portable backups and protect access to the browser profile.
